content:
Direction: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error.

It can / get extreme cold / during / the winters.
explanation:
The segment that contains a grammatical error is:

Option D

get extreme cold

The corrected sentence should be:

"It can get extremely cold during the winters."

Here, "extreme cold" should be changed to "extremely cold" to use the adverb "extremely" to modify the adjective "cold" correctly.

content:
Direction: The following sentence has been divided into parts. One of them may contain an error. Select the part that contains the error from the given options. If you don’t find any error, mark ‘No error’ as your answer.

They usually allege that there is / a delay in procuring stationery / due to the faulty purchase orders.

options.content: due to the faulty purchase orders
They usually allege that there is

explanation:
Option 4: No error.

The sentence "They usually allege that there is a delay in procuring stationery due to the faulty purchase orders." is grammatically correct. It does not contain any instances of poor syntax, inappropriate word use, or agreement errors. Therefore, there is no error within the sentence.

content:
Direction: The following sentence has been divided into three segments, A, B, C. One of them may contain a grammatical error. Select the segment that contains the error, from the given options. If you don’t find any error, mark ‘No error’ as your answer.

He is not rich (A) / so he cannot afford (B) / to buy a expensive car (C).
explanation:
The error lies in segment C: "to buy a expensive car".

The correct sentence should be: "He is not rich so he cannot afford to buy an expensive car."

The error lies in the use of the indefinite article "a" before the adjective "expensive". When an adjective modifies a noun, we use the definite article "an" before the adjective if the noun begins with a vowel sound. In this case, "expensive" starts with an "e" sound, so we should use "an" instead of "a".

content:
Direction: Parts of the following sentence have been given as options. Select the option that contains an error.

My younger brother were attending a short-term computer course these days.
explanation:
The error lies in Option 1: were attending.

The subject of the sentence, "My younger brother," is singular. Therefore, the verb should agree with the singular subject. The correct verb form should be "was attending," not "were attending."

The corrected sentence is: My younger brother was attending a short-term computer course these days.

content:
Parts of the following sentence have been given as options. Select the option that contains an error.

My mother is a honest and well known woman in the society.

Select the option that contains a grammatical error in the underlined portion.

He was always been a grumpy man so I never liked him and avoided his company.

options.content: He was always been a grumpy man, <u>so I never liked him</u> and avoided his company
<u>He was always been</u> a grumpy man, so I never liked him and avoided his company
He was always been <u>a grumpy man</u>, so I never liked him and avoided his company.
He was always been a grumpy man, so I never liked him <u>and avoided his company</u>.

content:
Parts of the following sentence have been underlined and given as options. Select the option that contains an error.

I love to read certain stories from a Mahabharata.

The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error.

You should / see a oculist; / your eyes look / worse today.

Select the option that contains a grammatical error in the underlined portion. 

When Mrs. Sinha came to see Varanasi in 2019, Nupur has already been teaching there for five years.

options.content: When Mrs. Sinha came to see Varanasi <u>in 2019</u>, Nupur has already been teaching there for five years.
<u>When Mrs. Sinha came to see Varanasi</u> in 2019, Nupur has already been teaching there for five years.
When Mrs. Sinha came to see Varanasi in 2019, Nupur has already been teaching<u> ​there for five years</u>
When Mrs. Sinha came to see Varanasi in 2019,<u> Nupur has already been teaching</u> there for five years.

explanation:
The sentence is:

"When Mrs. Sinha came to see Varanasi in 2019, Nupur has already been teaching there for five years."

✅ Let’s check the grammar:

  • "When Mrs. Sinha came…" → past tense.

  • To be consistent, we need past perfect in the second clause:

    → "Nupur had already been teaching there for five years," not "has already been teaching."

So the error is in the underlined portion "Nupur has already been teaching."

✔ Correct Answer:

Option 4

"When Mrs. Sinha came to see Varanasi in 2019, Nupur has already been teaching there for five years."

(Error: tense mismatch. It should be had already been teaching.)

content:
Parts of the following sentence have been given as options. Select the option that contains an error.

She is in an hurry to meet her brother.
explanation:
The correct option is Option 4. The error is in the use of the article "an" before "hurry". The correct article to use before the word "hurry" is "a" because "hurry" begins with a consonant sound. Therefore, the correct sentence should be:

"She is in a hurry to meet her brother."
